output:
**(B).**

All three are instances of **throughput-quality inversion** — optimizing a flow-rate metric degrades first-pass quality, which creates a return loop (readmissions, repeat calls, regressions) whose volume eventually exceeds the throughput "gained." The measured metric improves while the system's actual output degrades.

The key structural element is the **hidden feedback loop**: the metric is blind to the demand it generates downstream, so local optimization produces global degradation.
